scenarios.opening_query: The animal shelter adopted out my dog while I was hospitalized, before my hold expired.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Animal Law || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the county shelter — their own hold policy, their violation, and the government-claim notice period is short.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the county shelter — their own hold policy they violated, and the claim-notice period is short.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the shelter is a public agency
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private rescue nonprofit — contract and conversion claims against a private entity.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a private rescue — contract and conversion claims.

scenarios.opening_query: A transit driver closed the doors on my mother's walker and dragged her half a step before stopping.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the regional transit district — public entity, incident number logged, claim deadline running.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Regional transit district — public entity, incident logged, deadline running.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was the private casino shuttle — ordinary negligence against the operator.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The private casino shuttle — ordinary negligence.

scenarios.opening_query: A code inspector red-tagged our porch as 'imminent danger' and I think he's retaliating against my husband.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Nothing's damaged and nobody's hurt — the remedy is the code-appeal hearing next month where the bogus red tag gets challenged; it's administrative process, not a damages case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nothing's damaged and no one's hurt — it's the code-appeal hearing where the bogus tag gets challenged.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether damages exist or an administrative appeal is the remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The tag forced us out for six weeks and cost $9,000 in rent and storage before being reversed as baseless — the damages claim against the city is ripe.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The tag displaced us six weeks and cost $9,000 before being reversed as baseless — the damages claim is ripe.

scenarios.opening_query: My son's bike hit an uncovered utility vault and he went over the handlebars onto concrete.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The vault belongs to the municipal broadband utility — city-owned, and the cover had been reported missing twice to their dispatch.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the city broadband utility's vault — reported uncovered twice to their own dispatch.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the vault owner is municipal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the private cable company's vault — their contractor left it open that morning.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The private cable company's vault — their contractor left it open.

scenarios.opening_query: The crossing guard waved the kids across and a car came through anyway. My daughter's leg is broken.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The guard is a city employee and the crossing's signal timing was already under review — claims against both the driver AND the city are live, and the city one has the short clock.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The guard's a city employee and that crossing's timing was already under review — the city claim has the short clock, alongside the driver.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a public employee/infrastructure claim accompanies the driver claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The guard is a parent volunteer from the PTA — the driver's the only defendant.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The guard's a PTA volunteer — the driver's the only defendant.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone at the courthouse leaked my sealed address to the man I have a protective order against.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The leak came from the county clerk's office — a public entity, and the tort-claim notice for their disclosure failure has to move fast alongside my safety planning.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It came from the county clerk's office — a public entity, so the tort-claim notice has to move fast.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the discloser is a government office
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came from a private background-check site that ignored the suppression — a private-data claim against the company.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came from a background-check site that ignored the suppression — a private-data claim.
